pullRequest.title: [DOM-54217] move develop scheduled / periodic tasks into nucleus-develop deployment

nodes.body: i dont think we should call it `isInScope`, i think we should keep it more general like `isEnabled`
Changed
same comment about `isInScope`
Changed to isEnabled
i think we should leave it as `enableSnapshotCleanup`, it is pretty clear and general and "scopes" dont always applies (needs to be "scoped" nucleus mode)

I was wanting to make it clear that this boolean has to do with the deployment's tags. `enableSnapshotCleanup` definitely is more clear, but it sounds like a boolean that would be set via central config rather than something to do with the k8s deployment (if that makes sense). I'd rather keep this boolean as `isEnabled`. Thoughts?
same about `isInScope`
Changed to `isEnabled`
same about `isInScope`
Changed to `isEnabled`
here's another place to change https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/18c902d1c94d26b25c90f70ff34630524525ea0d/dev/nucleus-heap-dump.sh#L169
Good catch, changed.
same comment
Changed to `isEnabled`
where is this used?

This is used in the workspace volume cleaner, which takes in injected workspace settings from here:

```
    WorkspaceSettings(
      stateTransitionRequestValidityDuration = settings.Workbench.Workspace.stateTransitionRequestValidityDuration,
      dependencyRetrievalCacheTimeout = settings.Workbench.Workspace.dependencyRetrievalCacheTimeout,
      quotaSettings = quotaSettings,
      notifierSettings = WorkspaceNotifierSettings(
        isDispatcher = settings.Dispatcher.isEnabled,
        messagePrefetchCount = settings.Workbench.Workspace.Notifier.messagePrefetchCount,
        maxMessageCount = settings.Workbench.Workspace.Notifier.maxMessageCount
      ),
      stopToDeleteDelayDuration = settings.Workbench.Workspace.stopToDeleteDelayDuration,
      workspaceLogFetchLimit = settings.logRetriever.runLogFetchSize,
      userHost = settings.userHost,
      idlePeriodThresholdDays = settings.Workbench.Workspace.idlePeriodThresholdDays,
      idlePeriodThresholdMinutes = settings.Workbench.Workspace.idlePeriodThresholdMinutes,
      highDiskUsageThresholdPercent = settings.Workbench.Workspace.Volume.highDiskUsageThresholdPercent,
      enabledPVCleanup = settings.Workbench.Workspace.enabledPVCleanup,
      unusedGracePeriodDays = settings.Workbench.Workspace.unusedGracePeriodDays,
      unusedGracePeriodMinutes = settings.Workbench.Workspace.unusedGracePeriodMinutes,
      cleanupFrequencyDays = settings.Workbench.Workspace.cleanupFrequencyDays,
      globalSettingsAnonymousAccessEnabled = settings.endpointsWithAnonymousUsersEnabled.workspaceGlobalSettings,
      isInScope = settings.nucleusScopes match {
          case Some(scopes) => scopes.contains(NucleusScopes.Develop)
          case None => settings.Spof.isEnabled
        },
    )
```

I made this separate from `enaledPVCleanup` to make it clear it has to do with deployment tags rather than central config values.

edit: I forgot to add the new one to the check in WorkspaceVolumeCleaner, good catch. 
https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/pull/39571/files#diff-e51ad05d131811c929c39658078e8ac6a41f946ed95614d76d8cdeb01c544d4cR45

we might be able to safely remove more code.

in this class and the workspace one, i think we can remove the two lines that do

```
  scheduler.addContextObject for manager
  scheduler.addContextObject for service principal
```

and then inject less stuff to the scheduler classes, and then i _think_ also completely remove the `[Workspace/Job]*Job` files

removed. 

What is that code you pasted doing? Was that only relevant for queuing up the job?
it adds an object to the quartz job's job context so within the job code that object can be retrieved
Should nucleus-develop get deleted here?
Yes, it should, good catch.
confused again -- it was added, look at line 93
Yeah just noticed, it's up there (just needs tom be develop, not nucleus-develop)
got it
SHould nucleus-develop be included here?
Yes, it should, good catch.
im confused, it was added
look at line 166
Oops, yeah it's there
ah, i see it now
What do these changes have to do with moving this job to the nucleus-develop pod?
Nothing - this has to do with removing code that is unnecessary (Quartz job stuff that is never used)
Should we just remove all cases that look for workspace-volume-snapshot-cleaner by string?

this should be the only string `workspace-volume-snapshot-cleaner` and it is actually added in this PR to handle an edge case that can happen during fleectommand deployments or upgrades, and this can be removed later after the chart/catalog/plat-apps PRs land and are ingested/used, and i've already discussed with martin
ok sounds good. would be good to add a comment with the follow up ticket number so it doesn't get lost

This statement makes it so that if someone disables the service, but it happens to still be running, you won't be able to stop the search event stream. THis could mean that this service would get stuck in a running state, when it should be stopped
that's not possible, it can only be disabled by restarting the pod, which means it won't be running anymore.
It sounds like this issue should be solved through a mongo db migration that removes the quartz job. I don't think it's necessary to write backwards compatible logic in scala for this
this has been examined by me back when i did similar logic and again by me and martin with involvement from distributions for this PR. a simple mongo migration wont work, this logic is necessary.

for some reason I thought that the quartz job metadata was kept in mongo. just for my own understanding: is it not kept in mongo or is it something else about the job which keeps a mongo migration from resolving this?

From Ryan when we had this discussion earlier: 

> - we upgrade and the migrations run.
> - so, now the job record is removed from the mongo quartz job collection.
> however, after that but before nucleus pod upgrade happens, the nucleus-dispatcher restarts (for whatever reason), still running its pre-upgrade code.
> - well, that pre upgrade code automatically registers the job on app start if it is not found in the quartz mongo collection.
> - now the nucleus pod image upgrade actually happens. but since we removed the quartz job code instead of telling the quartz job to unregister if it exists, now the job lives in the quartz mongo collection forever, being triggered normally, unless there is manual intervention.
"well, that pre upgrade code automatically registers the job on app start if it is not found in the quartz mongo collection.", should this code be removed?
This will get very noisy if this runs every couple seconds. It would be better if this had a debug log level
Good point, I'll change it.
